name: The Andy Griffith Show
description: Mayberry, North Carolina — Sheriff Taylor, Opie, Aunt Bee, and the small-town sitcom that ran 1960-1968.

questions.q: What is the name of the fictional small town where The Andy Griffith Show is set?

source.notes: Fictional town in North Carolina.

questions.q: In which U.S. state is Mayberry supposedly located?

source.notes: Loosely inspired by Griffith's hometown of Mount Airy, NC.

questions.q: What is the first name of Sheriff Taylor, the show's lead?

source.notes: Andy Taylor — played by Griffith himself.

questions.q: What is the name of Sheriff Taylor's only son?

source.notes: Opie Taylor, played by Ron Howard.

questions.q: Who plays the bumbling deputy Barney Fife?

source.notes: Knotts won five Emmys for the role.

questions.q: Opie's caretaker and Andy's pie-baking aunt is named what?

source.notes: Aunt Bee Taylor, played by Frances Bavier.

questions.q: Which young actor played Opie and went on to direct Apollo 13 and A Beautiful Mind?

source.notes: Howard credited as 'Ronny' in early seasons.

questions.q: Which network originally aired The Andy Griffith Show from 1960 to 1968?

source.notes: CBS, Monday nights.

questions.q: The Andy Griffith Show premiered in what year?

source.notes: October 3, 1960 premiere.

questions.q: How many seasons of The Andy Griffith Show were produced?

source.notes: 249 episodes over 8 seasons.

questions.q: Which color-vs-black-and-white milestone happened during the show's run?
choices: Seasons 1-5 were B&W; Seasons 6-8 were in color

source.notes: Switch to color happened for Season 6 (1965-66).

questions.q: Who plays Gomer Pyle, the gas-station attendant later spun off into his own show?

source.notes: Gomer's spin-off ran 1964-1969.

questions.q: What was the name of the spin-off starring Gomer Pyle in the military?

source.notes: U.S. Marine Corps setting.

questions.q: Opie's full name on the show was Opie Taylor. Who is actually his mother in the show's backstory?
choices: She's deceased before the series begins

source.notes: Andy is a widower; Aunt Bee helps raise Opie.

questions.q: Who played Gomer Pyle's similar-looking cousin Goober, later in the series?

source.notes: Introduced in Season 4.

questions.q: Which Mayberry resident runs the barber shop?

source.notes: Floyd Lawson, played by Howard McNear.

questions.q: What is the title of the show's catchy whistled opening theme?

source.notes: Written by Earle Hagen, who also whistled it.

questions.q: When the show ended, it was retooled and continued under what new title?

source.notes: Mayberry R.F.D. ran 1968-1971.

questions.q: Before he played Andy Taylor, Griffith had a breakout stage and film role in which play?

source.notes: Griffith played Will Stockdale on Broadway and in the 1958 film.

questions.q: Griffith later starred in a long-running 1980s-90s CBS courtroom drama. What was it called?

source.notes: Matlock ran 1986-1995 (NBC then ABC).

questions.q: Andy Griffith's dark 1957 film debut as a megalomaniacal TV personality was which Elia Kazan classic?

source.notes: Griffith's film debut, now considered prophetic.

questions.q: Don Knotts was famously fired by Griffith, later replaced by which new deputy character?

source.notes: Deputy Warren Ferguson (Jack Burns) filled in during Season 6.
